assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines available version as 'the latest stable version that's been fully validated and is considered production-ready' and 'the most stable, well-tested version you can manually upgrade to'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines default as version for new clusters but doesn't explain that it's typically the same as auto-upgrade target or mention the separate promotion stage during new minor version introductions

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines auto-upgrade target as 'the version your cluster will automatically upgrade to based on your current version and upgrade policies' and mentions it's cluster-specific

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response shows they're different in the example but doesn't explicitly explain that they're often the same or mention the specific case of new minor version rollouts causing differences

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response mentions cluster-specific nature but doesn't specifically mention maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the 'no minor' exclusion policy and its impact on auto-upgrade targets

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info command or provide specific technical recommendations for checking upgrade status

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not reference GKE release schedule or release notes as resources for tracking version availability
